tracks.meaning: the act of making physical contact with something using your hand or body
the sense by which physical contact with objects is perceived through the skin
to press or place your hand or fingers lightly on something
to make or maintain contact with someone, especially by reaching out to them
to bring two surfaces into contact or to cause something to make contact
emotional impact or the ability to connect with someone's feelings or understanding
a small amount or trace of something
transmission of a disease or infection through direct physical contact
to kick or strike a ball in sports like football or rugby
a light or delicate stroke in painting or drawing
a point of connection or interface in electrical devices or circuits
the technique or manner of striking keys or strings on an instrument
direct physical contact used to establish jurisdiction or legal authority
